context:
Section 9 (Concluding remarks), page 33 (PDF pages 33/37 in the provided text), second open-question paragraph beginning with "Second,"

Similarly, strong duality is only established for convex \(h\) and affine cost, and we wonder whether it also holds in more general situations.
title: Strong duality (Kantorovich-type) for distorted optimal transport beyond affine costs
latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal A,\mathbb P)\) be an atomless probability space and let \(\mathcal X\) be the set of bounded real-valued random variables on it. A distortion function is an increasing function \(h:[0,1]\to[0,1]\) with \(h(0)=0\) and \(h(1)=1\). For \(Z\in\mathcal X\), define the distorted expectation
\[
\mathbb E_h[Z]=\int_0^\infty h(\mathbb P(Z>x))\,dx+\int_{-\infty}^0\big(h(\mathbb P(Z>x))-1\big)\,dx.
\]
Fix two Borel probability measures \(\mu,\nu\) on \(\mathbb R\), and a measurable cost function \(c:\mathbb R^2\to\mathbb R\) such that \(c(X,Y)\in\mathcal X\) for every coupling \((X,Y)\) with \(X\sim\mu\) and \(Y\sim\nu\). Define the primal distorted optimal transport value
\[
P_{h,c}(\mu,\nu)=\inf\{\mathbb E_h[c(X,Y)] : X\in\mathcal X,\ Y\in\mathcal X,\ X\sim\mu,\ Y\sim\nu\}.
\]
Define the constraint set of dual potentials
\[
S_c=\{(\varphi,\psi): \varphi,\psi:\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\ \text{measurable and bounded, and }\varphi(x)+\psi(y)\le c(x,y)\ \forall x,y\in\mathbb R\}.
\]
Let \(X_\mu\) and \(X_\nu\) be random variables with laws \(\mu\) and \(\nu\), respectively. Define the dual value
\[
D_{h,c}(\mu,\nu)=\sup_{(\varphi,\psi)\in S_c}\Big\{\mathbb E_h[\varphi(X_\mu)]+\mathbb E_h[\psi(X_\nu)]\Big\}.
\]
Weak duality \(P_{h,c}(\mu,\nu)\ge D_{h,c}(\mu,\nu)\) holds under assumptions given in the paper, and equality is proved in the paper when \(c\) is affine, i.e., \(c(x,y)=\alpha+\beta x+\gamma y\). The problem is to determine general conditions on \(h\), \(c\), \(\mu\), and \(\nu\) under which strong duality holds, i.e.,
\[
P_{h,c}(\mu,\nu)=D_{h,c}(\mu,\nu),
\]
and, if possible, to characterize when dual maximizers \((\varphi,\psi)\in S_c\) and primal optimal couplings \((X,Y)\) exist.
location_in_paper: Section 9 (Concluding remarks), page 33 (PDF pages 33/37 in the provided text), second open-question paragraph beginning with "Second,"
open_problem_quote: Similarly, strong duality is only established for convex \(h\) and affine cost, and we wonder whether it also holds in more general situations.
